name: EDM Drop Production
description: Classic drop-based EDM workflow from idea to mastered stem. Focused on energy management and clean low end.
steps:
1. Sketch the drop first — supersaw lead or bass riff over kick+snare
2. Build tension: 8/16-bar build with riser, snare roll, gradual filter open
3. Arrange intro/breakdown around the drop, not the other way around
4. Sidechain bass/pads to kick for pumping feel
5. Mix: mono bass below 120 Hz, wide leads, tight drum bus
6. Master with limiter targeting -8 LUFS for streaming
7. Reference against a commercial track in the same sub-genre
tools_required: DAW, supersaw synth (Serum/Vital), compressor with sidechain, limiter, reference track plugin

name: Hip-Hop Beat Construction
description: Sample-forward or melody-first beat-making workflow. Prioritizes groove, drum punch, and 808 sub management.
steps:
1. Pick BPM (70–85 for modern trap, 85–95 for boom-bap half-time)
2. Find the melodic hook: sample flip, chopped loop, or keyboard sketch
3. Program drums: kick on 1, snare/clap on 3, hi-hat patterns with swing
4. Lay the 808 — tuned to the root of the sample, glide between notes
5. Sidechain the 808 to the kick so they don't fight
6. Add ear candy: vocal chops, risers, reverse hits between sections
7. Mix: punchy drums, sub-clean 808, sample sits in the midrange
tools_required: DAW, drum sampler, 808 kit, sample chopping tool, saturation plugin

name: Cinematic Trailer Cue
description: Epic orchestral-meets-hybrid workflow for trailer-style cues with clear build/hit/aftermath structure.
steps:
1. Establish the core theme in 4–8 bars — simple, emotional, memorable
2. Build orchestration from minimal (piano/solo strings) to full ensemble
3. Layer hybrid sound design: sub drops, braams, reverses under orchestral hits
4. Rhythm bed: taiko + low percussion in the build, full toms + snare in the climax
5. Automate low-end and reverb sends heavily for the cinematic 'breathe'
6. Use impacts at every structural boundary (intro→build→hit→aftermath)
7. Master wide but mono-compatible; trailers often play on laptop speakers
tools_required: Orchestral library (Spitfire/Cinesamples), cinematic FX library, convolution reverb, heavy sub synth, transient designer

name: Indie Pop Song Arrangement
description: Vocal-centric songwriting arrangement pattern — intro, verse, pre-chorus, chorus, bridge. Tight and singable.
steps:
1. Write chord progression and vocal melody first, before any production choices
2. Arrange sections: intro (8) → verse (16) → pre-chorus (8) → chorus (16) → verse (16) → pre (8) → chorus (16) → bridge (8) → double chorus (32)
3. Production rises section by section: add elements, don't remove them
4. Drum program: simple kick/snare/hat in verse, full kit + percussion in chorus
5. Vocal production: doubles in chorus, harmonies in bridge, ad-libs in outro
6. Reference mix at multiple volumes — if the hook loses impact quiet, it's too compressed
7. Final master targets -10 LUFS for streaming distribution
tools_required: DAW, pitch correction (Melodyne/Autotune), vocal compressor, analog-emulating EQ, reverb send

name: Lo-fi Hip-Hop / Chillhop
description: Relaxed, imperfect-on-purpose workflow. Emphasizes texture, tape saturation, and a loose human feel.
steps:
1. Record or find a mellow jazz/soul chord loop (Rhodes, upright bass, sax)
2. Slow it down slightly (-1 to -3 semitones) to create lo-fi character
3. Program drums with intentional swing — shift hats off-grid for human feel
4. Apply tape saturation to the full mix for gentle compression and warmth
5. Add vinyl crackle layer at low volume for texture
6. Bitcrush or low-pass the mix bus subtly — roll off above 15 kHz
7. Keep arrangement simple; 4–8 bar loop variations for the full track
tools_required: DAW, tape saturation plugin, vinyl texture sample, bitcrusher, Rhodes/jazz sample library
